title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Message Lifecycle
summary: A fleet-control CLI calls an inventory service through a cache and gateway, but operators cannot tell whether failures come from naming, transport, status, or decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go message lifecycle increment in a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate a resource from the selected representation and its metadata, content, and current validity. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Message Lifecycle · Theory Message Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go fleet evidence model request G-4 crosses a resolver, TLS connection, gateway, cache, and origin before a changed payload is rejected. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
